metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2163
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.5: "Postsynaptic SNAP-47 KD Impairs LTP without Altering Basal Synaptic Transmission or Constitutive Recycling"
Fig.6: "SNARE-Dependent Interaction Is Required for SNAP-47's Role in LTP"

Literal: "These findings suggest that SNAP-47 specifically participates in the fusion machinery that is responsible for AMPAR exocytosis during LTP.". Experimental description: - Dissociated cultures: "Dissociated hippocampal cultures were prepared from newborn C57BL/6 mice"
- Slice recordings: "CD1 (Charles River) mice (8-12 g) were prepared for stereotactic injection using standard procedures approved..."
- Species of the overexpressed protein is not stated in the manuscript.
- Annotation to mouse species is made based on the shRNA experiments in mouse neurons.
